an1.394:1.1: "If a *bhikkhu* develops the first *jhāna* even for the duration of a finger-snap, *bhikkhus*, this one is called:
an1.394:1.2: 'a *bhikkhu* who dwells not bereft of *jhāna*, who does what the Teacher instructs, who follows counsel, who does not eat the alms of the realm in vain.'
an1.394:1.3: What then to say of those who cultivate it extensively?"
an1.395:1.1: "If a *bhikkhu* develops the second *jhāna* even for the duration of a finger-snap …
an1.398:1.1: develops the liberation of mind [*citta*] that is *mettā* …
an1.399:1.1: develops the liberation of mind [*citta*] that is *karuṇā* …
an1.400:1.1: develops the liberation of mind [*citta*] that is *muditā* …
an1.401:1.1: develops the liberation of mind [*citta*] that is *upekkhā* ….
an1.402:1.1: Dwells contemplating body in body, ardent, clearly comprehending [*sampajānā*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed covetousness and grief with regard to the world …
an1.405:1.1: dwells contemplating *dhammas* in *dhammas*, ardent, clearly comprehending [*sampajānā*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed covetousness and grief with regard to the world.
an1.406:1.1: Generates desire, strives, arouses energy, exerts the mind [*citta*], and makes effort for the non-arising of unarisen bad, unwholesome *dhammas*;
an1.407:1.1: generates desire, strives, arouses energy, exerts the mind [*citta*], and makes effort for the abandoning of arisen bad, unwholesome *dhammas*.
an1.408:1.1: Generates desire, strives, arouses energy, exerts the mind [*citta*], and makes effort for the arising of unarisen wholesome *dhammas*;
an1.409:1.1: generates desire, strives, arouses energy, exerts the mind [*citta*], and makes effort for the maintenance, non-disappearance, growth, expansion, development [*bhāvanā*], and fulfillment of arisen wholesome *dhammas*.
an1.410:1.1: Develops the basis for psychic power endowed with the *samādhi* of intention and *saṅkhāras* of striving …
an1.411:1.1: develops the basis for psychic power endowed with the *samādhi* of energy and *saṅkhāras* of striving …
an1.412:1.1: develops the basis for psychic power endowed with the *samādhi* of mind [*citta*] and *saṅkhāras* of striving …
an1.413:1.1: develops the basis for psychic power endowed with the *samādhi* of investigation and *saṅkhāras* of striving ….
an1.439:1.1: Having an internal *saññā* of form [*rūpa*], one sees external forms [*rūpa*] that are limited, of beautiful and ugly appearance.
an1.440:1.1: … Having an internal *saññā* of form [*rūpa*], one sees external forms [*rūpa*] that are unlimited, of beautiful and ugly appearance.
an1.441:1.1: … Without an internal *saññā* of form [*rūpa*], one sees external forms [*rūpa*] that are limited, of beautiful and ugly appearance.
an1.442:1.1: … Without an internal *saññā* of form [*rūpa*], one sees external forms [*rūpa*] that are unlimited, of beautiful and ugly appearance.
an1.443:1.1: … Without an internal *saññā* of form [*rūpa*], one sees external forms [*rūpa*] that are blue, blue in color, blue in appearance, blue in radiance.
an1.444:1.1: … Without an internal *saññā* of form [*rūpa*], one sees external forms [*rūpa*] that are yellow, yellow in color, yellow in appearance, yellow in radiance.
an1.445:1.1: … Without an internal *saññā* of form [*rūpa*], one sees external forms [*rūpa*] that are red, red in color, red in appearance, red in radiance.
an1.446:1.1: … Without an internal *saññā* of form [*rūpa*], one sees external forms [*rūpa*] that are white, white in color, white in appearance, white in radiance.
an1.448:1.1: without an internal *saññā* of form [*rūpa*], one sees external forms [*rūpa*] …
an1.449:1.1: one is intent solely on the beautiful …
an1.450:1.1: by completely transcending *saññās* of form [*rūpa*], by the disappearance of *saññās* of resistance, by non-attention to *saññās* of diversity, [perceiving] 'space is infinite,' one enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite space …
an1.451:1.1: by completely transcending the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite space, [perceiving] 'consciousness is infinite,' one enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite *viññāṇa* …
an1.452:1.1: by completely transcending the dimension [*āyatana*] of infinite *viññāṇa*, [perceiving] 'there is nothing,' one enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of nothingness …
an1.453:1.1: by completely transcending the dimension [*āyatana*] of nothingness, one enters and dwells in the dimension [*āyatana*] of neither-*saññā*-nor-non-*saññā* …
an1.454:1.1: by completely transcending the dimension [*āyatana*] of neither-*saññā*-nor-non-*saññā*, one enters and dwells in the cessation of *saññā* and *vedanā* ….
an1.467:1.1: develops the *saññā* of repulsiveness in food …
an1.468:1.1: develops the *saññā* of non-delight in the whole world …
an1.470:1.1: develops the *saññā* of *dukkha* in what is impermanent [*aniccā*] …
an1.478:1.1: develops the *saññā* of repulsiveness in food …
an1.479:1.1: develops the *saññā* of non-delight in the whole world …
an1.495:1.1: Develops the *indriya* of faith accompanied by the first *jhāna* …
an1.565:1.1: develops the *indriya* of faith accompanied by *upekkhā* …
an1.574:2.2: 'a *bhikkhu* who dwells not bereft of *jhāna*, who does what the Teacher instructs, who follows counsel, who does not eat the alms of the realm in vain.'
an1.574:2.3: What then to say of those who cultivate it extensively?"
